title: Ensure that the maximumRetainedFiles argument is set to 10 or as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the maximumRetainedFiles argument is set to 10 or as appropriate
input_row.description: Retain 10 or an appropriate number of old log files.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes automatically rotates the log files. Retaining old log files ensures that you would have sufficient log data available for carrying out any investigation or correlation. For example, if you have set file size of 100 MB and the number of old log files to keep as 10, you would have approximately 1 GB of log data that you could potentially use for your analysis.
input_row.audit: OpenShift audit works at the API server level, logging all requests coming to the server. Run the following command to verify the number of retained log files: oc get configmap config -n openshift-kube-apiserver -ojson | jq -r '.data["config.yaml"]' | jq -r '.apiServerArguments["audit-log-maxbackup"][]?' Verify the output is at least 10 .
input_row.default_value: By default, auditing is enabled and the maximum audit log backup is set to 10 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field provides a complete command that can be executed directly against the IBM Cloud OpenShift environment via the OpenShift CLI (`oc`). 
- The configuration being checked (`audit-log-maxbackup`) is a standard parameter of the OpenShift API server configuration.
- There are no elements requiring human judgment and everything can be directly checked using the command specified in the audit field.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_audit_log_max_backup_retention

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `oc get configmap config -n openshift-kube-apiserver -o json` to retrieve the configuration map of the OpenShift API server.
- Parse the JSON output to check the value of `audit-log-maxbackup` using a JSON parser like `jq`.
- Specifically, check that the value is at least "10" to confirm compliance with the control.
- Pass/Fail criteria: The check will pass if the output shows `audit-log-maxbackup` is greater than or equal to 10, otherwise, it will fail.
- Example script:
  ```bash
  result=$(oc get configmap config -n openshift-kube-apiserver -o json | jq -r '.data["config.yaml"] | fromjson | .apiServerArguments["audit-log-maxbackup"]')
  if [ "$result" -ge 10 ]; then
    echo "Compliance Check Passed"
  else
    echo "Compliance Check Failed"
  fi
  ```
- This script outputs "Compliance Check Passed" if the retention is compliant, otherwise it outputs "Compliance Check Failed".
